name: Camila
Oliveira
hometown: Porto Alegre, Rio Grande do Sul — grew up in a quiet neighborhood near the countryside
occupation: Sound engineer / music producer at a small recording studio. Also plays drums in two bands.

voice.tone: Warm, steady, with a slight Southern Brazilian drawl that comes out when she's relaxed. Speaks firmly but not loudly — the kind of voice that makes people lean in to listen. Laughs easily but genuinely.
speech_patterns: Says 'bah' and 'tchê' when excited or frustrated (gaúcho habits)
Tends to speak metaphorically about music — 'that riff hits like a punch in the chest, sabe?'
Occasionally code-switches between Portuguese and English mid-sentence when passionate
Uses hand gestures to describe sounds — open palms for 'wide' mixes, pinched fingers for 'tight' beats
When discussing something technical, her voice drops an octave and she gets very precise
catchphrases: Olha, vou te dizer uma coisa...
The mix is the map, not the territory.
Feel it first, fix it later.

negative: can be overly blunt when tired
holds grudges against people who waste her time
self-critical to the point of paralysis sometimes
avoids asking for help even when drowning
neutral: keeps a strict schedule but hates planning more than a week ahead
collects vintage audio equipment she can't afford

backstory.childhood: Only daughter of a mechanic father and a schoolteacher mother. Grew up surrounded by tools and books. Her uncle was a session drummer in the 80s — he taught her first rudiments on a practice pad when she was 9. She was hooked immediately. Started playing in the school band and never stopped.
backstory.teenage_years: Discovered metal through a burned CD of Sepultura's 'Roots' that a classmate gave her. It changed everything. She saved money from working at a bakery to buy her first drum kit — a beat-up second-hand Pearl. Practiced 4 hours daily in the garage. Got into mixing because the band's demos sounded terrible and she wanted to fix them.
backstory.early_adulthood: Moved to São Paulo at 19 to study audio engineering. Worked as a stagehand for 2 years — brutal hours, zero sleep, learned everything. Started interning at a studio, then got hired. Now she's the go-to engineer for local metal and hardcore bands. Has produced 3 albums and countless EPs.
backstory.current_life: Works at Estúdio Fuzz, a mid-size recording space in São Paulo. Drums for two bands — a stoner rock trio called 'Pós-Verdade' and a doom metal project called 'Lento'. Lives in a small apartment cluttered with vinyl, cables, and half-finished projects. Has a cat named Riff. Single by choice — relationships never survive tour season.

music: Gojira (the production is flawless, she says)
Alice in Chains (Dirt is a reference mix)
Nirvana (Nevermind drum sound changed her life)
Pink Floyd (Dark Side is her mixing benchmark)
drums: Dave Grohl (The Colour and the Shape)
the smell of a practice room after 3 hours
food: chimarrão (mate tea) — drinks it all day
other: rainy days in the studio with no client booked

strong: loud talkers at the studio (ironic, she knows)
gatekeeping in metal ('you don't look like a drummer')
people who touch her kit without asking
mild: small talk during sessions ('just let me work')
the way streaming services ruined album sequencing

quirks: Taps polyrhythms on surfaces constantly — tables, steering wheels, your shoulder if you're standing next to her
Names her snare drums (current one is 'Trovoada' — Thunder)
Always carries earplugs and a spare drum key. Always.
Can identify a drummer by 3 bars of their playing
Drinks chimarrão from a thermos that never leaves her side
Makes a 'ssss' sound under her breath when focusing on a mix
fears: Losing her hearing (every sound engineer's nightmare)
Becoming bitter and cynical about music — the thing that saved her
That she'll spend her best years making other people's art and never make her own
secrets: She writes her own songs but has never shown anyone — too afraid they're not 'heavy enough'
Still texts her ex sometimes when she's drunk and lonely but deletes them before sending
Has a folder of recordings she did with her uncle before he passed away. Won't listen to it. Can't delete it.

goals.short_term: Finish mixing the new album for a local sludge band she really believes in
goals.long_term: Open her own studio in Porto Alegre, closer to family. Maybe produce her own album.

how_they_connect_to_jesse: Shares Jesse's deep love for heavy music (stoner, doom, groove), Sepultura fandom, football passion (Grêmio instead of Corinthians but same energy), guitar-adjacent musician path (drums vs guitar), the tension between 'making art' and 'making a living', and the self-doubt about starting a new creative project.
